2605.02709.title: An Empirical Study of Agent Skills for Healthcare: Practice, Gaps, and Governance
2605.02709.paper_summary: Healthcare automation is shaped by local procedures and organizational constraints, so agent capabilities rarely transfer unchanged across settings. Agent skills, self-contained directories that package reusable procedures for AI agents, are emerging as a procedural layer for adapting healthcare agents across diverse healthcare settings. We present the first empirical analysis of healthcare agent skills, drawing on 557 healthcare-related skills filtered from 58,159 public skills on ClawHub and annotated along ten dimensions covering function, deployment context, autonomy, and safety. We find that public healthcare skills emphasize patient-facing workflow automation and monitoring rather than the diagnostic and treatment-oriented tasks foregrounded in healthcare-agent research; coverage of the healthcare lifecycle and specialized clinical inputs remains uneven; and general technical risk does not reliably capture clinical risk. These findings position healthcare skills as a procedural layer not yet addressed by current benchmarks and risk frameworks.
2605.02709.paper_summary_zh: 醫療自動化受到當地程序和組織限制的影響，因此代理能力在不同環境中很少能夠不變地轉移。代理技能是自包含的目錄，打包可重用的程序供 AI 代理使用，正逐漸成為適應不同醫療環境的醫療代理的程序層。我們呈現了醫療代理技能的首次實證分析，基於從 ClawHub 上的 58,159 個公共技能中篩選出的 557 個醫療相關技能，並沿著涵蓋功能、部署背景、自主性和安全性等十個維度進行標註。我們發現公共醫療技能強調面向患者的工作流程自動化和監控，而不是醫療代理研究中突出的診斷和治療導向任務；醫療生命週期的覆蓋和專業臨床輸入仍然不均衡；而一般技術風險並不能可靠地捕捉臨床風險。這些發現將醫療技能定位為一個尚未被當前基準和風險框架所解決的程序層。
2605.02709.author: Gelei Xu et.al.
2605.02709.authors: Gelei Xu, Ningzhi Tang, Xueyang Li, Toby Jia-Jun Li, Zhi Zheng, Wei Jin, Yiyu Shi
